description: Enhance task-mgr doctor with --setup flag to audit Claude Code settings (permissions, hooks, skills, CLAUDE.md, project config) and auto-fix issues.

criteria: Rust: No warnings in `cargo check` output
Rust: No warnings in `cargo clippy` output
Rust: All tests pass with `cargo test`
No breaking changes to existing doctor command behavior
globalAcceptanceCriteria.description: These criteria apply to ALL implementation tasks

priorityPhilosophy.description: The hierarchy of what matters most when implementing tasks
hierarchy: 1. PLAN — Approach before code. Anticipate edge cases. Consider 2-3 approaches, pick the best
2. PHASE 2 FOUNDATION — Extensible check registry so new checks can be added as functions without changing the orchestrator
3. FUNCTIONING CODE — Pragmatic, reliable code, wired in according to the plan
4. CORRECTNESS — Self-critique after code. Code compiles, type-checks, passes all tests deterministically
5. CODE QUALITY — Quality dimensions explicit. Clean code, good patterns, no warnings
principles: Quality dimensions explicit — read qualityDimensions on every task; know what 'good' looks like before coding
Approach before code — consider 2-3 approaches with tradeoffs, pick the best, then implement
Edge cases = test cases — every known edge case must have a corresponding test; boundaries are where bugs hide
Self-critique after code — review your own implementation for correctness, style, and performance before moving on
Ship solid working code with tests to prove it
Handle Option/Result explicitly; avoid unwrap() in production code

prohibitedOutcomes: Tests that only assert 'no crash' or check type without verifying content
Auto-fix that modifies ~/.claude/settings.json without explicit user confirmation
False positive checks that waste user time on correct configurations
Error messages that don't include a copy-pasteable fix command

priorityGuidelines.medium: 21-50: Fix in current phase if time permits

acceptanceCriteria: Test: SetupSeverity ordering (Blocker > Warning > Info > Pass)
Test: SetupCheck serializes to JSON with all fields
Test: SetupAuditResult correctly counts blockers/warnings/passing
Test: empty audit returns all-passing result
Known-bad discriminator: check with fix_command=None still serializes correctly
Tests compile (may use #[ignore] for unimplemented check functions)
userStories.description: Write tests for the new SetupCheck, SetupSeverity, SetupCategory types and the check registry pattern. Tests should verify severity classification, JSON serialization, and the audit orchestrator contract.
edgeCases: settings.json doesn't exist: check should report Info, not error
guard-destructive.sh has custom modifications: only check for LOOP_ALLOW_DESTRUCTIVE bypass, don't overwrite
Skills source dir doesn't exist in repo: skip skill installation, only warn about missing global skills
Settings.json has deny rules matching CODING_ALLOWED_TOOLS: report as Blocker
Worktree missing .claude/settings.local.json: report as Warning with suggestion
Multiple projects with different tool needs: recommend per-project config over global changes

failureModes.expectedBehavior: Report as Warning with parse error message, don't crash
Skip global checks, report as Warning

invariants: Audit never modifies any files (read-only operation)
Every check has a severity, message, and category
Blocker count + Warning count + Pass count = total checks
userStories.notes: Follow existing output.rs patterns (IssueType, Issue, Fix). The new types go in a new file src/commands/doctor/setup_output.rs or extend output.rs.

correctness: Types must serialize cleanly for --json output
style: Follow existing output.rs patterns: derive Serialize, Debug, Clone

userStories.description: Implement check functions in a new file src/commands/doctor/setup_checks.rs. Each function takes paths/config and returns a SetupCheck. Checks: defaultMode, deny conflicts, ask conflicts, hook bypass, skills installed, project config, CLAUDE.md.
userStories.notes: Import CODING_ALLOWED_TOOLS from loop_engine::config (make it pub if not already). Parse settings.json with serde_json::Value (don't need full typed struct — settings format may change). For hook check, read file as string and grep for the bypass pattern. Reference EXPECTED_GLOBAL_SKILLS from engine.rs.

correctness: Must never crash on missing/malformed files — graceful degradation
Must correctly identify defaultMode: 'default' as problematic
performance: Each check should return in <10ms (local file reads only)
style: Pure functions: path in, SetupCheck out. No side effects in check functions.

userStories.description: Wire the check functions into an audit_setup() orchestrator function. Add --setup flag to the Doctor CLI command. When --setup is passed, run setup checks. When neither --setup nor DB flags, run both.
userStories.notes: The orchestrator determines paths: ~/.claude/settings.json, ~/.claude/hooks/guard-destructive.sh, ~/.claude/commands/, .task-mgr/config.json, CLAUDE.md. Use dirs::home_dir() or std::env::var('HOME').

cargo test passes
userStories.description: Implement auto-fix functions for issues that can be repaired automatically: install missing skills, generate .task-mgr/config.json, patch guard-destructive.sh hook, generate template CLAUDE.md. Never auto-modify ~/.claude/settings.json.
userStories.notes: For hook patching: read the file, check if bypass already exists, if not insert after the #!/bin/bash line and before the first command. Create .bak backup before modifying. For project config auto-detection: check for .proto files -> add protoc, Dockerfile -> add docker, pyproject.toml -> add python3/uv, etc.

correctness: Hook patch must preserve existing checks
style: Each fix function is pure: takes inputs, returns result. Side effects (file writes) happen inside the function but are clearly documented.

userStories.title: Auto-fix engine for repairable issues

userStories.description: Add a lightweight setup pre-check at loop startup that runs only when starting a new task list (not resuming). Checks blocker-level issues only and prints a warning banner.
userStories.notes: Determine 'new vs resume' by checking if iteration_counter > 0 in global_state or if there's an existing run for this PRD. The check should be fast — only read settings.json and check defaultMode + deny rules. Skip skills/config/CLAUDE.md checks (those are for the full --setup audit).

correctness: Must correctly distinguish new task list from resume
Non-blocking — must not prevent loop from starting
style: Follow existing check_global_skills pattern in engine.rs for warning output

acceptanceCriteria: fix_patch_hook rejects symlink targets (returns failure SetupFix)
fix_install_skills rejects symlink destinations
fix_generate_project_config rejects symlink config path
fix_generate_claude_md rejects symlink CLAUDE.md path
Test: symlink at hook path returns success:false with descriptive message
Test: symlink at skill destination returns success:false
userStories.description: Add symlink safety checks to all auto-fix file write operations. Before writing, verify the target path is not a symlink using std::fs::symlink_metadata. Return a failure SetupFix with a clear message if a symlink is detected. This prevents auto-fix from following symlinks to write outside expected paths.
userStories.notes: Use std::fs::symlink_metadata(path).map(|m| m.file_type().is_symlink()) to detect symlinks without following them. Apply to: fix_patch_hook, fix_install_skills (dst), fix_generate_project_config, fix_generate_claude_md.
userStories.title: Add symlink safety checks to auto-fix file operations

acceptanceCriteria: Deny rule 'Bash' (no parens) detected as conflict when any Bash(x:*) is in allowed tools
Deny rule 'Bash(*)' detected as conflict
Exact match still works as before
Test: broader deny patterns produce Blocker checks
Test: unrelated broader patterns (e.g., 'FooTool') produce no checks
userStories.description: Extend check_deny_conflicts to detect broader deny patterns that would block allowed tools. Currently only exact matches are caught. A deny rule like 'Bash' blocks all Bash tools but isn't detected because it doesn't exactly match entries like 'Bash(cargo:*)'. Add prefix/pattern matching: if a deny rule is a prefix of any allowed tool (e.g., 'Bash' is a prefix of 'Bash(cargo:*)'), flag it as a conflict.
userStories.notes: Consider: deny 'Bash' should match any 'Bash(...)' in allowed tools. A simple startsWith check on the tool name prefix (before the '(' if present) would catch this. Be careful not to false-positive on unrelated tools.
userStories.title: Extend deny conflict detection to catch broader deny patterns

acceptanceCriteria: Extract run_all_setup_checks() helper called from audit_setup()
audit_setup() calls helper twice instead of duplicating 6-line check sequence
No behavior change — all existing tests pass
cargo clippy -- -D warnings passes
userStories.description: audit_setup() in mod.rs contains the same 6-line check-running sequence twice (lines 279-285 and 336-342). Extract a helper function run_all_setup_checks(settings_path, hook_path, commands_dir, db_dir, project_dir) -> Vec<SetupCheck> to eliminate this duplication. If a new check is added, both blocks must currently be updated in lockstep.
userStories.notes: The helper takes the 5 path arguments already computed in audit_setup() and returns Vec<SetupCheck>. Simple extraction — no signature changes needed.
userStories.title: Extract run_all_setup_checks() to DRY audit_setup()

acceptanceCriteria: Extract read_settings_json(path) -> Result<Value, SetupCheck> helper in setup_checks.rs
check_default_mode and check_deny_conflicts use the shared helper
check_default_mode body under 30 lines after extraction
check_deny_conflicts body under 30 lines after extraction
No behavior change — all existing tests pass
cargo clippy -- -D warnings passes
userStories.description: check_default_mode (76 lines) and check_deny_conflicts (49 lines) both independently read and parse settings.json with identical boilerplate: check exists → read_to_string → serde_json::from_str, each with its own error handling. Extract a shared helper read_settings_json(path) -> Result<Value, SetupCheck> (or similar) that handles the file-not-found, read-error, and parse-error cases once. This will shrink both functions well below 30 lines.
userStories.notes: The helper should return Result<serde_json::Value, Option<SetupCheck>> or similar. For check_default_mode, missing file = Pass; for check_deny_conflicts, missing file = empty Vec. Consider returning Option<Value> where None means 'no settings file, treat as pass'.
userStories.title: Extract shared settings.json read+parse helper

acceptanceCriteria: SetupCheckRegistry either removed or evolved to accept CheckContext
If removed: SetupCheckFn type alias also removed, tests removed
If evolved: audit_setup() uses the registry instead of direct calls
No dead code warnings — cargo clippy passes
Decision documented in commit message
userStories.description: SetupCheckRegistry and SetupCheckFn in setup_output.rs are defined and tested but never used in production code. The actual orchestrator (audit_setup in mod.rs) calls check functions directly because they need context (file paths) that fn() -> SetupCheck cannot provide. Either: (A) Remove the registry as dead code, or (B) Evolve to fn(&CheckContext) -> Vec<SetupCheck> and wire audit_setup to use it. Option B would make adding new checks a single-point change (register in one place).
userStories.notes: This is an architectural decision. Option A is simpler and removes ~40 lines of dead code + tests. Option B fulfills the original extensibility goal from the PRD ('extensible check registry so new checks can be added as functions without changing the orchestrator'). If choosing B, consider: struct CheckContext { settings_path, hook_path, commands_dir, db_dir, project_dir } and type SetupCheckFn = fn(&CheckContext) -> Vec<SetupCheck>.
userStories.title: Resolve unused SetupCheckRegistry — remove or evolve
